name: Pan-Seared Chicken Thighs with Garlic Green Beans and Mashed Potatoes
ingredients:
15g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
50g 3.8% Organic Nordic Milk
20g Organic Salted Butter
25g Garlic Scapes
120g Green Beans (frozen)
300g Not So Pretty White Potatoes
400g Boneless Chicken Thighs (4, frozen)
instructions:
• Preheat oven to 400°F.
• Season 14 oz chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
• Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe pan, sear thighs skin-side down for 5 minutes until golden.
• Flip and transfer to oven for 20 minutes.
• Meanwhile, boil 10.5 oz diced potatoes until tender, about 12 minutes.
• Steam 4.2 oz green beans for 5 minutes.
• Mash potatoes with 1.8 oz milk and 3/4 oz butter.
• Sauté steamed green beans with 1 oz minced garlic scapes for 2 minutes.
• Let chicken rest 5 minutes before serving with garlic green beans and creamy mashed potatoes.
tip:
Tip!: This classic comfort dinner features juicy chicken thighs with perfectly seasoned sides.
• The garlic scapes add a subtle, sweet garlic flavor that complements both the beans and potatoes
beautifully.
• A perfect Tuesday evening meal that feels like home!

name: Beef and Vegetable Rice Bowl
ingredients:
15g Garlic Scapes
15g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
15g Organic Tamari Sauce
60g Spinach
80g Mini Leafy Carrots
100g Green Zucchini
120g Organic White Basmati Rice
200g Lean Ground Beef (frozen)
instructions:
• Cook 1/2 cup basmati rice according to package directions.
• Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
• Add 7 oz ground beef and cook for 5-6 minutes, breaking it up as it browns.
• Add 3.5 oz diced zucchini and 2.8 oz mini carrots, cook for 4 minutes until vegetables start to soften.
• Stir in 2 oz spinach and 1/2 oz minced garlic scapes, cooking until spinach wilts.
• Season with 1 tbsp tamari sauce, salt, and pepper.
• Serve the beef and vegetable mixture over fluffy rice.
tip:
Tip!: This hearty rice bowl delivers complete nutrition in one satisfying dish!
• The combination of protein-rich beef and colorful vegetables creates a balanced meal that's both filling
and budget-friendly.
• Try adding a dollop of hot sauce or fresh herbs for extra flavor.

name: Pork Tenderloin with Garlic Mashed Potatoes and Green Beans
ingredients:
10g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
15g Étoiles d'Or Salted Butter
60g 3.8% Organic Nordic Milk
20g Organic Salted Butter
30g Garlic Scapes
150g Green Beans (frozen)
400g Not So Pretty White Potatoes
300g Pork Tenderloin (frozen)
instructions:
• Preheat oven to 400°F.
• Season 10.5 oz pork tenderloin with salt and pepper, sear in 1 tbsp oil for 3 minutes per side until
golden.
• Transfer to oven for 15 minutes.
• Meanwhile, boil 14 oz diced potatoes until tender, about 12 minutes.
• Steam 5.3 oz green beans for 6 minutes until crisp-tender.
• Mash potatoes with 2 oz milk, 3/4 oz butter, and 1 oz minced garlic scapes.
• Season with salt and pepper.
• Let pork rest 5 minutes before slicing.
• Drizzle with remaining oil and serve with garlic mashed potatoes and green beans.
tip:
Tip!: This classic comfort dinner features tender pork with creamy garlic-infused potatoes.
• The garlic scapes add a mild, sweet garlic flavor that's more delicate than regular garlic.
• A perfect Saturday evening meal that feels special while staying within budget!

name: Turkey and Vegetable Pasta Salad
ingredients:
30g Green Vinaigrette
10g Organic Dijon Mustard
20g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
60g Spinach
80g Mini Leafy Carrots
100g Small Coloured Tomatoes
150g Artisan Maccheroni
180g Lean Ground Turkey (frozen)
instructions:
• Cook 5.3 oz maccheroni pasta according to package directions, drain and cool.
• In a large bowl, combine cooked pasta with 6.3 oz cooked ground turkey, 3.5 oz halved cherry
tomatoes, 2.8 oz diced mini carrots, and 2 oz fresh spinach leaves.
• Whisk together 4 tsp olive oil, 2 tsp Dijon mustard, and 2 tbsp green vinaigrette.
• Toss pasta mixture with dressing until well coated.
• Let stand 5 minutes for flavors to meld.
• Serve chilled or at room temperature.
tip:
Tip!: This protein-packed pasta salad is perfect for your Saturday lunch!
• The combination of lean turkey and fresh vegetables creates a satisfying meal that's both nutritious and
budget-friendly.
• Feel free to add any leftover herbs for extra freshness.

name: Herb-Crusted Salmon with Roasted Sweet Potatoes
ingredients:
15g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
100g Yellow Onions
10g Étoiles d'Or Salted Butter
15g Organic Salted Butter
10g Santo Cilantro
20g Prospera Genovese Basil
300g Sweet Potatoes
240g Atlantic Salmon Fillet (frozen)
instructions:
• Preheat oven to 425°F.
• Cut 10.5 oz sweet potatoes into 1-inch cubes and toss with 1 tbsp olive oil and salt.
• Roast for 20 minutes.
• Meanwhile, mix 3/4 oz fresh basil, 1/3 oz cilantro, 1/2 oz butter, and pinch of salt to make herb crust.
• Pat 8.5 oz salmon fillets dry and spread herb mixture on top.
• Add salmon to oven with potatoes for final 8 minutes.
• Sauté 3.5 oz sliced onions in remaining oil until caramelized, about 6 minutes.
• Serve salmon over sweet potatoes with caramelized onions alongside.
tip:
Tip!: This elegant dinner showcases omega-rich salmon with naturally sweet roasted vegetables.
• The fresh herb crust adds restaurant-quality flavor while keeping costs reasonable.
• A squeeze of lemon would brighten this dish beautifully!

name: Chicken and Vegetable Stir-Fry with Rice
ingredients:
10g Organic Tamari Sauce
15g Spicy Garlic Oil
120g Organic White Basmati Rice
40g Spinach
60g Li Ren Baby Bok Choy
80g Mini Leafy Carrots
100g Green Zucchini
150g Yellow Onions
200g Chicken Breast (frozen)
instructions:
• Cook 1/2 cup basmati rice according to package directions.
• Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
• Add 7 oz diced chicken breast and cook for 5-6 minutes until golden.
• Add 1 cup sliced onions and 3.5 oz zucchini pieces, stir-fry for 3 minutes.
• Toss in 2.8 oz mini carrots and 2 oz baby bok choy, cooking for 2 more minutes until vegetables are
tender-crisp.
• Add 1.4 oz spinach and cook until wilted.
• Season with 2 tsp tamari sauce and 1 tbsp garlic oil.
• Serve immediately over fluffy rice.
tip:
Tip!: This colorful stir-fry delivers complete nutrition in under 30 minutes!
• The combination of lean protein and fresh vegetables creates a satisfying meal that fits perfectly within
your budget.
• Try adding a pinch of ginger or red pepper flakes for extra warmth.

name: Turkey and Spinach Quesadillas with Tomato Salad
ingredients:
5g Organic Dijon Mustard
10g Organic Salted Butter
15g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
80g Small Coloured Tomatoes
60g Mild White Cheddar
80g Spinach
2g Traditional Tortillas (10", pack of 6, frozen)
150g Lean Ground Turkey (frozen)
instructions:
• Cook 5.3 oz ground turkey in a dry pan over medium heat, breaking it up as it cooks, about 6 minutes.
• Season with salt and pepper.
• Wilt 2.8 oz spinach in the same pan for 1 minute.
• Let cool slightly.
• Place filling and 2 oz grated cheddar on one half of each tortilla, fold over.
• Cook quesadillas in 1/2 tbsp butter for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cheese melts.
• Meanwhile, dice 2.8 oz cherry tomatoes and toss with 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp Dijon mustard.
• Cut quesadillas into wedges and serve with fresh tomato salad.
tip:
Tip!: These protein-packed quesadillas make a perfect Monday lunch!
• The combination of lean turkey, iron-rich spinach, and melted cheese creates a satisfying meal.
• The fresh tomato salad adds a bright, acidic contrast that balances the richness beautifully.

name: Beef Stir-Fry with Mixed Vegetables and Noodles
ingredients:
20g Garlic Scapes
15g Extra-Virgin Tunisian Olive Oil
15g Organic Tamari Sauce
60g Mini Leafy Carrots
80g Li Ren Baby Bok Choy
100g Green Zucchini
120g Yellow Onions
200g Ramen Noodles (frozen)
250g Stir-Fry Beef Strips (tenderized, frozen)
instructions:
• Cook 7 oz ramen noodles according to package directions, drain and set aside.
• Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large wok or pan over high heat.
• Add 8.8 oz beef strips and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until browned.
• Add 4.2 oz sliced onions and cook 2 minutes.
• Toss in 3.5 oz zucchini pieces, 2.8 oz baby bok choy, and 2.1 oz mini carrots.
• Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
• Add cooked noodles, 1 tbsp tamari sauce, and 3/4 oz minced garlic scapes.
• Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until heated through.
• Serve immediately while hot.
tip:
Tip!: This hearty stir-fry brings together tender beef and fresh vegetables in a satisfying Sunday dinner.
• The garlic scapes add a wonderful aromatic touch that elevates this simple dish.
• Perfect for ending your weekend on a delicious note!

name: Chicken Caesar Wrap with Fresh Vegetables
ingredients:
10g Organic Salted Butter
25g Organic Caesar Dressing & Marinade
40g Mini Leafy Carrots
60g Small Coloured Tomatoes
80g Romaine Lettuce
2g Traditional Tortillas (10", pack of 6, frozen)
160g Chicken Breasts (frozen)
instructions:
• Season 5.6 oz chicken breast with salt and pepper.
• Cook in 1/2 tbsp butter over medium heat for 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
• Let rest, then slice thinly.
• Wash and chop 2.8 oz romaine lettuce.
• Dice 2.1 oz cherry tomatoes and 1.4 oz mini carrots.
• Warm 2 large tortillas briefly in a dry pan.
• Spread 1.5 tbsp Caesar dressing on each tortilla.
• Layer with lettuce, sliced chicken, tomatoes, and carrots.
• Roll tightly, tucking in sides.
• Cut in half diagonally and serve immediately.
tip:
Tip!: These fresh wraps are perfect for your Sunday lunch!
• The combination of tender chicken and crisp vegetables with creamy Caesar dressing creates a
satisfying handheld meal.
• Try adding a sprinkle of parmesan or croutons for extra texture and flavor.
